# DSET project settings and manifest.
#
# This is the sole canonical DSET project configuration. It combines
# project identity and topology with operator-controlled behavior so
# discovery never has to reconcile two competing configuration owners.
# Repository-local governing documents still define the policies and
# values that these settings are allowed to select.
#
# All settings below are optional at read time and show their executable
# defaults explicitly. Keep the explicit values when copying this file so a
# reviewer does not need to know parser fallbacks. Unknown settings fail
# validation, which catches misspellings instead of silently ignoring them.
#
# New DSET writers use only the unique `dset_settings.toml` carrier inside
# `.dset`. Competing settings carriers or nested project roots fail closed.
#
# Settings schema implemented by this file.
# Accepted for new writers: "1.5".
schema_version = "1.5"
canonical_command = "python -m dset_toolchain verify ."

[artifacts]
# Include the optional direct artifact subtype after the primary artifact type
# in IDs and filenames for newly emitted artifacts only.
#
# Accepted:
# - false (default): type-only structural name.
#   Example: DSET-VERSION-001-0-4-core.md
#   Inside the artifact: artifact_type = "version" and
#   artifact_subtype = "version_scope".
# - true: add the direct subtype token.
#   Example: DSET-VERSION-VERSION-SCOPE-001-0-4-core.md
#
# Existing IDs and filenames are immutable and are never renamed when this
# value changes. The setting affects the carrier name only; it does not change
# an atomic record's separate semantic Type/subtype.
#
# Registered artifact subtype examples:
# - analysis_report: solution_landscape, root_cause_analysis, proposal,
#   technical_investigation, external_audit_analysis
# - specification: domain_model, behavior, architecture, design, governance
# - procedure: playbook, runbook
# - plan: implementation_plan, test_plan, evaluation_plan
# - version: roadmap, version_scope, change, release_plan, readiness_record,
#   release_record
# - implementation: source_code, documentation, configuration, migration,
#   test_implementation, evaluation_implementation
# - evidence_record: test_result, evaluation_result, review_report, run_record
# - derived_view: project_overview, health_dashboard, traceability_index,
#   changelog
# - navigation: readme, hub, index
# atomic_record and verification currently have no artifact subtypes.
subtype_in_names = false

# Set the gate used before a new immutable atomic artifact is emitted.
#
# Accepted:
# - "medium" (default): require clear authority, one primary claim, semantic
#   Type, owning scope, provenance, material links, priority, and acceptance.
#   Optional non-authoritative context may remain explicitly unknown.
# - "high": also require every material boundary, lineage, conflict, and proof
#   question to be precise. DSET asks focused questions or stops without writing
#   until the atom can safely remain immutable.
#
# Both values check whether the claim may belong one enabled scope higher and
# propose eligible promotion. Promotion always requires operator acceptance.
creation_strictness = "medium"

[workflows.implement]
# Choose what dset-implement may do before implementation begins.
#
# Accepted:
# - "lazy" (default): reconcile missing session Decisions, then create or update
#   separate Test/Evaluation plans and the implementation plan as needed before
#   implementing. Completed prerequisites are skipped.
# - "strict": perform implementation only from already sufficient accepted
#   artifacts. Missing or ambiguous inputs stop; DSET does not create or repair
#   Decisions or plans and never falls back to lazy mode.
#
# Example lazy chain:
# decisions -> compile when required -> plan-proof -> plan-implementation -> implement
mode = "lazy"

[compilation]
# Semantic compilation is performed by dset-compile only when requested
# or required by a downstream entry gate. New atoms do not force it.
mode = "on_demand"
skill = "dset-compile"
runtime_output = ".dset_runtime/generated"

[changes]
# Select the default Git workspace for a newly created Change when the caller
# does not request a mode explicitly.
#
# Accepted:
# - "integration-branch" (default): place the Change on the project's configured
#   integration branch, normally dev. The protected branch, normally main,
#   remains the base for delivery.
# - "branch-worktree": create an isolated dset/<change-slug> branch/worktree
#   based on the integration branch. This is useful for concurrent Changes.
#
# An explicit per-Change request overrides this default. The project manifest
# still owns the actual integration and protected branch names.
default_workspace = "integration-branch"

[delegation]
# Select the registered multi-agent breadth budget. The selected profile changes
# exploration/review breadth, never the accepted scope, safety boundary, or
# required Tests/Evaluations. Operator instructions override the profile.
#
# Accepted profiles are defined by
# `010_dset-skill-budget.toml`:
# - "low": 0-1 subagents, depth 1, one round.
# - "medium" (default): target 2 and maximum 3 subagents, depth 1, two rounds.
# - "high": 4-6 subagents, depth 2, three rounds.
#
# Subagents inherit the main session's model and reasoning effort by default.
# A cheaper token price alone is not evidence of lower total task cost.
budget_profile = "medium"

[priority]
# Define the project-wide priority vocabulary from highest to lowest.
#
# Accepted: a TOML array containing at least two unique strings.
# Default: the five values below. Array position is precedence: critical
# outranks high, and deferred ranks last.
#
# Priority is available to every governed artifact. It orders current attention
# but does not override external authority, lifecycle state, proof quality,
# dependencies, or authorization gates.
scale = ["critical", "high", "medium", "low", "deferred"]

# Priority assigned when a governed artifact has neither a direct value nor an
# inherited value from its canonical owner.
#
# Accepted: one value present in priority.scale. Default: "medium".
default = "medium"

# Project identity, topology, and executable repository boundaries.

[project]
key = "DSET"
id = "dset-specs-loops-framework"
name = "DSET Spec Loops"
repository_slug = "dset-specs-loops-framework"
repository_role = "framework-source-and-adopter"

[supportability]
status = "applicable"
reason = "GitHub Actions and repository rules operate the production delivery path from dev to main"
authority = "github"
runbook = "000_dset-ops-supportability-delivery-runbook.md"

[release]
status = "applicable"
integration_branch = "dev"
protected_branch = "main"
publisher = "github"
tag_pattern = "v{product_version}"

[work_items]
atomic_scopes = ["project-control", "dset-meta", "dset-gov", "dset-tool", "dset-skill", "dset-ops"]

[structure]
layout = "separated-methodology-v1"

[profiles]
runtime_risk = "low-risk-hosted-automation"
durability_topology = "external-platform"
enforcement = "python-v1"
artifact = "documentation-v1"
repository_governance = "core-v1"

[change_contract]
change_id_format = "project-type-layer-sequence"
change_slug_format = "kebab-case"
pull_request_required_before_archive = true
archive_requires_fresh_verification = true
keep_pull_request_draft_until_archive_ready = true

# Migrated canonical settings; this section is not an artifact ledger.
[artifact_catalog]
schema_version = "1.0"
profile = "documentation-v1"

[artifact_catalog.classification]
unclassified = "error"
multiple_matches = "error"

[artifact_catalog.classification.direct_metadata_fields]
type = "artifact_type"
subtype = "artifact_subtype"

[[artifact_catalog.exclusions]]
pattern = ".gitignore"
rationale = "Git transport metadata is not a development artifact."

[[artifact_catalog.exclusions]]
pattern = "LICENSE"
rationale = "The repository license is an external legal instrument."

[[artifact_catalog.exclusions]]
pattern = "THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md"
rationale = "Third-party notices mirror external legal attribution."

[[artifact_catalog.exclusions]]
pattern = "*_dset-project-licenses-*.txt"
rationale = "Vendored third-party license texts remain external legal sources."

[[artifact_catalog.exclusions]]
pattern = "uv.lock"
rationale = "The generated dependency lock is classified through its owning package configuration."

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "atomic_record"
primary_question = "What atomic project claim or directive does this carrier preserve?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = []

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "analysis_report"
primary_question = "What interpreted understanding should inform, but not authorize, a decision?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["solution_landscape", "root_cause_analysis", "proposal", "technical_investigation", "external_audit_analysis"]

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "specification"
primary_question = "What current or required truth does this document compile?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["domain_model", "behavior", "architecture", "design", "governance"]

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "procedure"
primary_question = "What reusable way of working should an operator enact?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["playbook", "runbook"]

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "plan"
primary_question = "What intended work or proof should occur for this bounded objective?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["implementation_plan", "test_plan", "evaluation_plan"]

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "version"
primary_question = "What bounded version intent, transaction, gate, or publication record does this artifact own?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["roadmap", "version_scope", "change", "release_plan", "readiness_record", "release_record"]

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "implementation"
primary_question = "What realizes accepted truth or QA definitions?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["source_code", "documentation", "configuration", "migration", "test_implementation", "evaluation_implementation"]

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "evidence_record"
primary_question = "What was observed for an identified subject, revision, method, and time?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["test_result", "evaluation_result", "review_report", "run_record"]

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "verification"
primary_question = "What do the applicable evidence records support at this revision?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = []

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "derived_view"
primary_question = "What does current canonical project data imply for this declared use?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["project_overview", "health_dashboard", "traceability_index", "changelog"]

[[artifact_catalog.artifact_types]]
id = "navigation"
primary_question = "Where should a reader start or continue?"
allow_empty_subtype = true
subtypes = ["readme", "hub", "index"]

description: Turn ambiguous product or domain intent into decision-ready DSET vocabulary, boundaries, invariants, requirements, scenarios, proof obligations, alternatives, and remaining unknowns. # DSET Clarify

This is the thin wrapper for `domain-clarification`; resolved repository-local documents own substantive behavior.

## Resolve

1. Invoke the installed shared runtime exactly once with `dset skills context --skill dset-clarify --target TARGET --objective OBJECTIVE --llm-session-id LLM_SESSION_ID [--session-id SESSION_ID]`. Replace the host-session placeholder; reuse the explicit DSET `SESSION_ID` from a prior handoff and omit it only on initial DSET entry. Stop when the ID, launcher, or command is unavailable, and never select an alternate runtime.
2. Verify and report the returned repository/Work Area, skill/workflow/mode, wrapper, ordered rule identities and digests, ruleset identity, conflict coverage, and run/session identity.
3. Read the returned project-owned rule documents in order. Stop on an identity mismatch, unresolved conflict, required unavailable conflict coverage, or any returned stop status.
4. Retain `run_id`, `session_id`, and `repository_root`. For an active specialist transition, invoke `dset runtime handoff RUN_ID REPOSITORY_ROOT --next-signal WORKFLOW`, return the same `session_id`, and require the next context call to pass it. Only a true session completion or stop invokes `dset runtime finish RUN_ID REPOSITORY_ROOT --status TERMINAL_STATUS`; report command failure.

## Handoff

Return decision-ready alternatives and remaining unknowns. Route any authorized write through the resolved artifact owner and maintenance disposition; never edit an emitted atomic artifact. Return the session identity when available, recommend the next handoff, and stop before consequential selection or implementation.

---
name: dset-compile
description: Semantically reconcile accepted atomic DSET artifacts into affected evergreen specifications and plans under the target repository's local governance. Use when the operator requests compilation, pending atoms must become current project truth, or implementation, verification, or release entry criteria require current evergreen artifacts; do not run merely because one new atom exists.
---

# DSET Compile

This is the thin wrapper for semantic compilation. Repository-local governance
owns the substantive compilation and authoring rules.

## Resolve

1. Invoke the installed shared runtime exactly once with `dset skills context
   --skill dset-compile --target TARGET --objective OBJECTIVE
   --llm-session-id LLM_SESSION_ID [--session-id SESSION_ID]`. Reuse an active
   DSET session ID. Stop if the runtime, target, identity, or governed workflow
   is unavailable.
2. Verify the returned repository/Work Area, `compile` workflow, ordered rule
   identities and digests, ruleset identity, conflict coverage, and run/session
   identity.
3. Read the returned project-owned rules in order. Stop on an identity mismatch,
   unresolved governing conflict, or a returned stop status.

## Compile

1. Inventory accepted atomic artifacts beyond each affected evergreen owner's
   recorded frontier. Do not treat a new atom as an automatic compilation
   trigger.
2. Resolve `replacement_of`, `override_of`, `child_of`, conflict resolution,
   and lifecycle state before synthesis. Never edit an atomic artifact.
3. Update only affected evergreen specifications or plans. Synthesize current
   consequences in the target document's vocabulary and structure; do not
   concatenate atom text or expose a chronological decision log.
4. If a project reference still exactly matches distributable framework truth,
   retain its portable TOML reference. If the project consequence diverges,
   replace the reference with a project-owned evergreen document.
5. Record the considered atomic frontier by scope and semantic type. A frontier
   marks considered inputs, not proof that the synthesis is correct.
6. Report updated owners, unchanged owners, pending atoms, conflicts, material
   unknowns, and session provenance. Stop before implementation, testing,
   evaluation, or release work unless the caller separately requested it.

## Continuity

For a chained workflow, hand off the active run with `dset runtime handoff
RUN_ID REPOSITORY_ROOT --next-signal WORKFLOW` and preserve the same session ID.
Only terminal completion or a governed stop invokes `dset runtime finish`.

This is the bounded rootless initialization wrapper. It owns no project rules and cannot replace the new repository-local authority it creates.

## Preview and initialize

1. Invoke the installed shared runtime exactly once with `dset skills context --skill dset-init --target TARGET --objective OBJECTIVE --llm-session-id LLM_SESSION_ID`. Replace the placeholder with the current host session ID; require `status: initialization-required` and stop on an unavailable ID/launcher, project root, competing roots, or command failure.
2. Collect explicit target, project key, project ID, name, license, profile, source, repository identity when known, package, and any Work Areas. Do not infer a different source or profile after preview.
3. Run `dset init TARGET --project-key KEY --project-id ID --name NAME --license LICENSE --profile PROFILE --source SOURCE --format json` without `--execute`. Report source identity, every planned path, refusal conditions, and that no write occurred.
4. Without explicit write authorization, return the exact preview and stop. With authorization, run the same invocation once with `--execute`; never overwrite or merge an existing destination.
5. Report materialized paths and validation result. Stop after any failure or successful validation; the next invocation must resolve the new local governance.

## Stop

Return initialization identity, preview or execution status, diagnostics, and the next `$dset` handoff. Never make a product Decision, create implementation work, or continue into another lifecycle mode.
